zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Henry Wing於1530年出生在英國英格蘭亨廷格多史爾亨廷格頓時，他的父親Mathew Wing22歲，母親Marie 'Mary' Gulliver21歲。他和Elizabeth Cordie or Cordray至少有1個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1590年3月24日在英國盧特藍德諾斯 盧費納姆享年60歲。

nameMeanings.name: Wing
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Weng (Old West Scandinavian Wenge, perhaps originally a nickname from vengi ‘pillow, cushion’).

English and Scottish: habitational name from either Wing in Buckinghamshire or Wing in Rutland. The Buckinghamshire placename probably means ‘the people of Wiwa’, from an Old English personal name Wiwa + the Old English groupname suffix -ingas. The Rutland placename and probably derives from Old Norse vengi ‘field’.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 榮, see   Rong   4.

variants.name: Rong
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Guang, Fang, Shuhong, Xiao, Zhao, Bai, Chen, Cheng, Ding, Guan, Guo, Han.
In some cases also Norwegian: habitational name from a farm name in Hordaland related to rong ‘stern (of a boat)’.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 容, meaning ‘tolerate’ or ‘appearance’ in Chinese: (i) from Rong (容), the name of an ancient state (location unknown). (ii) from Rong (容), the name of a post in ancient China held by officials in charge of the management of rituals, ceremonies, and music. (iii) from the personal name of Rong Cheng (容成), an official during the reign of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ), who was said to be the inventor of the first Chinese calendar. (iv) from the personal name of Zhong Rong (仲容), an official during the reign of the legendary Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ). (v) from Zi Rong (子容), style name of Nan Rong Shi (南容適), also called Nan Rong (南容), a student of Confucius (551–479 BC ) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Henry Wing was born in 1530, in Huntingdon, Huntingdonshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Mathew Wing, was 22 and his mother, Marie 'Mary' Gulliver, was 21. He had at least 1 son and 2 daughters with Elizabeth Cordie or Cordray. He died on 24 March 1590, in North Luffenham, Rutland, England, at the age of 60.

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Henry Wing(이)가 1530년 영국 잉글랜드 헌틴그도실르 헌틴그던에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Mathew Wing(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Marie 'Mary' Gulliver(은)는 21세였다. 그는 Elizabeth Cordie or Cordray(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1590년 3월 24일 영국 루틀랜드 노르스 루펜함에서 60세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Henry Wing родился в 1530 г. в местности хантингдон, хантингдоншир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Mathew Wing было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Marie 'Mary' Gulliver, 21.
